title: Charlie Work

jokes.text: Frank, pick up! Pick up, buddy, pick up, pick up, pick up, pick up, pick up!
jokes.setup: Charlie making an urgent phone call
jokes.punchline: Excessive repetition of 'pick up' shows his frantic desperation
jokes.explanation: Establishes Charlie's anxiety and manic personality through simple repetition. Functional character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Frank, pick up! Pick up, buddy, pick up, pick up, pick up, pick up, pick up!

jokes.text: Oh, shit.
jokes.setup: Charlie realizes the gang is already at the bar despite his instructions
jokes.punchline: Simple defeated reaction to his worst case scenario happening
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words convey complete defeat. Simple but effective.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, shit.

jokes.text: No time to explain it to you, you wouldn't get it anyway.
jokes.setup: Charlie asks about chickens everywhere
jokes.punchline: Mac's condescending dismissal despite being involved in absurd scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac arrogance - acting superior while doing something ridiculous. Good character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No time to explain it to you, you wouldn't get it anyway.

jokes.text: Carmine's: A Place for Steaks.
jokes.setup: Dennis painting a fake restaurant sign
jokes.punchline: The generic, uninspired slogan for their fake steakhouse
jokes.explanation: Beautifully generic fake restaurant name and slogan. Gets funnier when you think about it.

dialogue.line: Carmine's: A Place for Steaks.

jokes.text: Yeah, we get a passing grade because I bust my ass to make sure that happens, okay?
jokes.setup: Others dismissive of health inspection importance
jokes.punchline: Charlie taking excessive pride in basic bar hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's misplaced professional pride in questionable hygiene standards.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, we get a passing grade because I bust my ass to make sure that happens, okay?

jokes.text: Charlie, we found a loophole in the system. Yeah, it's a closing loophole; It involves airline miles and points.
jokes.setup: Explaining their scheme
jokes.punchline: Vague nonsensical explanation that sounds like corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Great parody of incomprehensible get-rich-quick schemes. 'Closing loophole' is perfect nonsense.

dialogue.line: Charlie, we found a loophole in the system. Yeah, it's a closing loophole; It involves airline miles and points.

jokes.text: Frank, Jesus Christ, man, where are your shoes? - I lost 'em in the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Charlie noticing Frank is shoeless
jokes.punchline: Frank casually admitting he lost shoes in bathroom
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the toilet joke payoff. Frank's casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Frank, Jesus Christ, man, where are your shoes?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I lost 'em in the bathroom.

jokes.text: It's not hilarious, it's just a stool with a nail sticking out of it.
jokes.setup: Dee calling the joke stool 'hilarious'
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact description of dangerous 'prank'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery highlighting how insane their 'joke' actually is. Perfect dark comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's not hilarious, it's just a stool with a nail sticking out of it.

jokes.text: Yeah, and when it pokes their butts, they scream, 'cause they just got a nail right in the butt.
jokes.setup: Charlie dismissing the stool as unfunny
jokes.punchline: Dee's enthusiastic explanation of why injuring customers is comedy gold
jokes.explanation: Dee's complete misunderstanding of comedy and casual attitude toward violence is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, and when it pokes their butts, they scream, 'cause they just got a nail right in the butt.

jokes.text: You flushed your shoes down the toilet? Why would you do that? It's an anxiety thing, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Frank's casual admission about losing shoes
jokes.punchline: Reveal that he deliberately flushed them due to 'anxiety'
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and perfectly Frank. The casual delivery of something so absurd is comedy gold.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You flushed your shoes down the toilet? Why would you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's an anxiety thing, Charlie.

jokes.text: Flushing things gives me control. It's a thing. - That's not a thing.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his flushing compulsion
jokes.punchline: Frank insisting it's legitimate, Charlie's immediate denial
jokes.explanation: Frank's conviction about his made-up disorder vs Charlie's disbelief creates perfect comedy tension.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Flushing things gives me control. It's a thing.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That's not a thing.

jokes.text: Oh, son of a bitch. Did you flush your shirt? - Yep.
jokes.setup: Frank's earlier shoe-flushing revelation
jokes.punchline: Frank has now escalated to flushing clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the flushing gag. Frank's casual 'Yep' makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, son of a bitch. Did you flush your shirt?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yep.

jokes.text: like, how can a whole group of people have the same idea at the same time? What, do they just say it all at once? It's ludicrous.
jokes.setup: Mac arguing about credit for the plan
jokes.punchline: His genuinely confused logic about group brainstorming
jokes.explanation: Mac's literal interpretation of collaborative thinking is perfectly obtuse and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: like, how can a whole group of people have the same idea at the same time? What, do they just say it all at once? It's ludicrous.

jokes.text: What happened to the glory hole cover? I took it off. I-I thought you... you put it on there by accident.
jokes.setup: Charlie noticing missing glory hole cover
jokes.punchline: Mac's innocent assumption that covering it was an accident
jokes.explanation: Casual mention of glory hole existence and Mac's oblivious helpfulness is peak Sunny dark comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What happened to the glory hole cover?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I took it off. I-I thought you... you put it on there by accident.

jokes.text: Oh, shit! This thing's tits!
jokes.setup: Frank discovering the vacuum sealer
jokes.punchline: Frank's crude enthusiasm for the machine
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank vulgarity and excitement. Functional character comedy but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, shit! This thing's tits!

jokes.text: Are you sure? Maybe he's a racist, too.
jokes.setup: Charlie telling Frank to take down 'Talk American' sign
jokes.punchline: Frank's hopeful assumption about shared bigotry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - casual racism and hope for kinship over bigotry. Dark but brilliantly written.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Are you sure? Maybe he's a racist, too.

jokes.text: Dee, shut up, shut up, shut up! That's, uh, G-sharp. Okay, I got it, I got it.
jokes.setup: Carbon monoxide detector beeping
jokes.punchline: Charlie identifying the alarm as a musical note instead of a warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - treating a safety alarm as a musical exercise. Shows his unique worldview.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Dee, shut up, shut up, shut up! That's, uh, G-sharp. Okay, I got it, I got it.

jokes.text: actually, what I do is I block the vents to the furnace and that fills the basement with dangerous gases. Drives the rodents away.
jokes.setup: Explaining why carbon monoxide detector is going off
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual admission of deliberately creating deadly conditions
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark - Charlie's matter-of-fact description of potentially lethal 'pest control' method.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: actually, what I do is I block the vents to the furnace and that fills the basement with dangerous gases. Drives the rodents away.

jokes.text: Mac was doing that thing where he tries to casually bring something up to me, like I don't notice. Come to me like a man!
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why he scratched Mac
jokes.punchline: Dennis's hyper-masculine demand for direct confrontation over trivial matters
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism and toxic masculinity over something completely minor.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Mac was doing that thing where he tries to casually bring something up to me, like I don't notice. Come to me like a man!

jokes.text: You want to eat here? Uh, yeah, you know, it's a long drive back to Ohio and I figured I was gonna be here a while, unloading all these steaks.
jokes.setup: Delivery guy wanting to eat at 'restaurant'
jokes.punchline: Customer wanting to dine at a fake steakhouse they're running a scam on
jokes.explanation: Great ironic situation - their fake restaurant scam backfiring when someone actually wants service.

dialogue.speaker: Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: You want to eat here?

dialogue.speaker: Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: Uh, yeah, you know, it's a long drive back to Ohio and I figured I was gonna be here a while, unloading all these steaks.

jokes.text: Four hundred? You ordered 4,000.
jokes.setup: Charlie thinking they ordered a reasonable amount
jokes.punchline: The delivery reveals their order is 10 times bigger than expected
jokes.explanation: Great escalation. The scale of their incompetence is revealed in one devastating line.

dialogue.speaker: Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: Four hundred? You ordered 4,000.

jokes.text: You guys used Frank's credit card, you bought a bunch of airline miles. You used those airline miles to purchase 400 steaks...
jokes.setup: Charlie claiming not to understand the plan
jokes.punchline: Charlie perfectly explaining their absurdly convoluted scheme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - Charlie understanding their ridiculous plan better than they do. Great structure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You guys used Frank's credit card, you bought a bunch of airline miles. You used those airline miles to purchase 400 steaks...

jokes.text: you know, we basically had already explained it to you, so...
jokes.setup: Charlie perfectly explaining their plan they claimed he wouldn't understand
jokes.punchline: Mac's weak attempt to save face after being shown up
jokes.explanation: Mac's pathetic attempt to maintain superiority after Charlie outsmarts him. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: you know, we basically had already explained it to you, so...

jokes.text: Or that Dee's oversized fingers could've accidentally pressed 'zero' again when she ordered the steaks online.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining plan flaws
jokes.punchline: Insulting Dee's fingers while identifying her mistake
jokes.explanation: Good insult comedy combined with plot exposition. The 'oversized fingers' is a nice touch.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Or that Dee's oversized fingers could've accidentally pressed 'zero' again when she ordered the steaks online.

jokes.text: Uh, this is an alley. I think I'd rather just sit inside.
jokes.setup: Charlie presenting alley as 'patio area'
jokes.punchline: Delivery guy's matter-of-fact observation of obvious reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight man response to Charlie's ridiculous spin on dining in an alley.

dialogue.speaker: Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: Uh, this is an alley. I think I'd rather just sit inside.

jokes.text: Alan was let go on account of his leniency.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking what happened to Alan
jokes.punchline: New inspector's ominous explanation about predecessor
jokes.explanation: Good setup for increased stakes. Creates tension about stricter enforcement.

dialogue.line: Alan was let go on account of his leniency.

jokes.text: I assume all the toilets are in working order. Oh, absolutely. Yeah, I wouldn't bother checking if I were you.
jokes.setup: Inspector asking about toilets
jokes.punchline: Charlie's obvious attempt to steer her away from the clogged toilet
jokes.explanation: Charlie's terrible attempt at misdirection. The more he protests, the more suspicious he sounds.

dialogue.speaker: Health Inspector
dialogue.line: I assume all the toilets are in working order.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, absolutely. Yeah, I wouldn't bother checking if I were you.

jokes.text: Do I smell cheese?
jokes.setup: Health inspector checking bathroom
jokes.punchline: Detecting cheese smell in a bathroom
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor about questionable bar hygiene. Simple but effective.

dialogue.line: Do I smell cheese?

jokes.text: Oh, no, he wants a turkey burger. - What? Why? - I don't know. I offered it to him.
jokes.setup: Charlie giving Dee a steak to serve
jokes.punchline: Dee randomly offering menu items they don't have
jokes.explanation: Dee's incompetence at playing restaurant waitress. Functional comedy but not standout.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, no, he wants a turkey burger.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I don't know. I offered it to him.

jokes.text: He looks like a turkey burger kind of guy?! Oh, my God, Dee
jokes.setup: Dee's explanation for offering turkey burger
jokes.punchline: Charlie's incredulous reaction to her 'reasoning'
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Dee's absurd logic about customer 'types'. Shows Charlie's exasperation.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He looks like a turkey burger kind of guy?! Oh, my God, Dee

jokes.text: Don't ask questions, just moan a bit, after a couple of moans say, 'I'm sorry.'
jokes.setup: Charlie giving Mac bathroom instructions
jokes.punchline: Specific directions for fake bathroom sounds
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - Charlie's detailed instructions for performance art bathroom sounds.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Don't ask questions, just moan a bit, after a couple of moans say, 'I'm sorry.'

jokes.text: Hey, buddy, how about a courtesy cough or something next time?
jokes.setup: Mac's fake bathroom performance
jokes.punchline: Charlie's elaborate public shaming of 'rude' bathroom behavior
jokes.explanation: Charlie's fake outrage performance for the inspector. Good cringe comedy setup.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hey, buddy, how about a courtesy cough or something next time?

jokes.text: I'm painting a shirt!
jokes.setup: Charlie telling Frank to find a shirt
jokes.punchline: Frank's literal interpretation - painting on fabric
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic - solving the shirt problem in the most impractical way possible.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm painting a shirt!

jokes.text: The steak is burnt to shit. It's not even what I ordered.
jokes.setup: Delivery guy's 'restaurant experience'
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of terrible service
jokes.explanation: Straightforward customer complaint comedy. Gets the job done but nothing special.

dialogue.speaker: Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: The steak is burnt to shit. It's not even what I ordered.

jokes.text: this is the worst restaurant I've ever been to.
jokes.setup: The gang's fake restaurant charade
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of their 'restaurant'
jokes.explanation: Ironic since it's not actually a restaurant at all. Simple but effective payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: this is the worst restaurant I've ever been to.

jokes.text: Give this to Frank. Tell him G-sharp. Then you got to paint him head to toe in black. Why would I do any of that?
jokes.setup: Charlie giving Dee increasingly bizarre instructions
jokes.punchline: Dee's reasonable question about painting Frank black
jokes.explanation: Charlie's plans are becoming increasingly unhinged. The G-sharp reference and paint instruction are wonderfully random.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Give this to Frank. Tell him G-sharp. Then you got to paint him head to toe in black.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Why would I do any of that?

jokes.text: I said bar, bar twice!
jokes.setup: Charlie getting nervous during inspection
jokes.punchline: Charlie's anxious self-awareness about verbal fumble
jokes.explanation: Good nervous Charlie moment showing his social anxiety. Character-appropriate fumble.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I said bar, bar twice!

jokes.text: A bit excessive? Yes, I know, but I'm a little anal when it comes to securing the perishables.
jokes.setup: Inspector seeing chickens and steaks in keg room
jokes.punchline: Charlie spinning criminal activity as professional diligence
jokes.explanation: Great Charlie spin work - presenting obvious scam evidence as fastidious food safety.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: A bit excessive? Yes, I know, but I'm a little anal when it comes to securing the perishables.

jokes.text: All right, all right, all right.
jokes.setup: Various moments of Dennis saying his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Inspector commenting that it 'says it all'
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for the running gag with external validation of how annoying it is.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All right, all right, all right.

jokes.text: The regulation is exactly six feet and you were at five and a half. Oh, that goddamn bitch!
jokes.setup: Earlier Dumpster positioning argument with Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee's defiance causing the only real violation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - Charlie's one legitimate concern becomes the only actual violation.

dialogue.speaker: Sandy
dialogue.line: The regulation is exactly six feet and you were at five and a half.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, that goddamn bitch!

jokes.text: Congratulations. Oh, my... You got me with the whole Dumpster thing!
jokes.setup: Charlie expecting failure due to all the chaos
jokes.punchline: Getting highest grade despite everything going wrong
jokes.explanation: Great ironic victory - Charlie's elaborate preparations work despite complete chaos.

dialogue.speaker: Sandy
dialogue.line: Congratulations.

dialogue.speaker: Sandy
dialogue.line: Oh, my... You got me with the whole Dumpster thing!

jokes.text: However, you do have an amazingly clean bar and therefore I'm giving Paddy's Pub the highest possible grade.
jokes.setup: Charlie thinking they failed because of the dumpster
jokes.punchline: Despite everything, they get the highest grade possible
jokes.explanation: Great subversion. After all the chaos and near-misses, Charlie's competence actually worked.

dialogue.speaker: Health Inspector
dialogue.line: However, you do have an amazingly clean bar and therefore I'm giving Paddy's Pub the highest possible grade.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, we don't, we don't give a shit about that.
jokes.setup: Charlie's pride in health inspection success
jokes.punchline: Gang's complete dismissal of his achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Charlie's moment. Shows how little they value his actual competence.

dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, we don't, we don't give a shit about that.

jokes.text: We always pass, okay? We never have a hard time passing. It's not a big deal. Nobody gives a shit. You didn't do anything that special.
jokes.setup: Charlie celebrating his success
jokes.punchline: Mac completely dismisses Charlie's heroic effort and competence
jokes.explanation: Crushing deflation of Charlie's moment. Mac's ingratitude after Charlie saved them is perfectly awful.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We always pass, okay? We never have a hard time passing. It's not a big deal. Nobody gives a shit. You didn't do anything that special.

jokes.text: That's how you make a joke stool right there! That's... Ow, Charlie!
jokes.setup: Earlier joke stool discussion
jokes.punchline: Dee stepping on nail stool, proving it's dangerous not funny
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - Dee gets her comeuppance for defending the dangerous 'prank'.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That's how you make a joke stool right there! That's... Ow, Charlie!

jokes.text: That's how you make a joke stool right there! That's... Ow, Charlie! Did you do that? Yeah, you know what? I think I did.
jokes.setup: Dee falling victim to her own dangerous stool
jokes.punchline: Charlie's quiet satisfaction at getting revenge for her dumpster failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff and character moment. Charlie's quiet revenge and Dee getting hurt by her own 'joke' is poetic justice.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That's how you make a joke stool right there! That's... Ow, Charlie! Did you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, you know what? I think I did.

tmdb_overview: Charlie goes into overdrive when he's notified that the health inspector will be arriving that day. Meanwhile, the rest of the gang is trying to exploit a flaw in the air-miles reward system.
